findings.evidence: PR line 60 has `theorem _root_.HilbertBasis.mapₗᵢ_orthonormal ... (e : E ≃ₗᵢ[𝕜] F) : Orthonormal 𝕜 (e ∘ b)`; Mathlib has `Orthonormal.comp_linearIsometry {v : ι → E} (hv : Orthonormal 𝕜 v) (f : E →ₗᵢ[𝕜] E') : Orthonormal 𝕜 (f ∘ v)` at `Mathlib/Analysis/InnerProductSpace/Orthonormal.lean:313`.
findings.fix: Remove this lemma, or restate any needed wrapper in terms of the existing general theorem `Orthonormal.comp_linearIsometry` without requiring `HilbertBasis`/surjectivity.
findings.issue: `HilbertBasis.mapₗᵢ_orthonormal` proves orthonormality only for `e : E ≃ₗᵢ[𝕜] F` and `b : HilbertBasis`, but the natural Mathlib statement works for any orthonormal family and any linear isometry.

summary: The transport primitive is at the requested level, but one added consumer theorem specializes an existing Mathlib theorem and unnecessarily requires a Hilbert basis and a surjective isometry.
